title: Hot Wheels XL
summary: 200px|rightHot Wheels XL is a 1:43 scale series of Hot Wheels; in essence the castings in this series are scaled up versions of mainline castings, carrying over their decos and often their wheels. This series was developed by Charlie Angulo.

title: Micro Hot Wheels
summary: Micro Hot Wheels is a series of miniature Hot Wheel cars that came out in 1989 to compete with the newest phenomenon of diecast toys "Micro Machines". They are the first series for Hot Wheels to venture into making cars smaller than their usual 1:64 scale. After the series ended within a year (or two?...

title: Red Edition
summary:
right|200x200px
The Red Editions are Target exclusive basic models. They share the same deco as the mainline cars, but have exclusive colors. Series began in 2017, with 12 releases per year in groups of three in each case.

title: List of unreleased Hot Wheels
summary: This is a list of unreleased Hot Wheels products. This article encompasses unreleased casting designs, playsets and other related products.

title: International Gold Editions
summary:
200px|right
In the spring of 2014 Mattel Germany and Mattel Holland held another Mail In promotion. This time the promotion car was a special Chrome Gold Quick n' Sik. After purchasing 5 Hot Wheels cars and sending the receipt to Mattel before April 15th 2014, you were sent a special Quick n' Sik on a long card but this time without a Kar Keeper.

title: Fake Hot Wheels
summary: Hot Wheels has been a popular diecast manufacture for many decades. Unfortunately, a lot of knock offs have been made. Whether it is a terrible error, or the use of similar Hot Wheels castings in different manufacturer brand, or if it is a toy that uses a familiar flame logo on the blister, it must be shared for all to laugh.
